RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT DOMENIC CANNIZZARO'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS Overruled with ten days to answer. (Weil & Brown 7:78-77:83.2). The first, eight and eleventh causes of action are adequately pled. Any further information that Domenic Cannizzaro desires about those claims can be obtained through discovery. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carlos Martinez of the Bay Area Law Firm (408.286.3070) for cross defendant and moving party; Clyde H. Charlton (485.2200) appearing via Court Call for cross complainant Ann Gervasi

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT FRED RICHARD'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (OF ANN GERVASI) is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 3rd cause of action, breach of contract. Cross complainant does not allege that cross-defendant Fred Richard was a party to the contract, and therefore he may not be liable for the breach. Cross-complainant may amend to allege a cause of action regarding the contract that is supported by the allegations. Demurrer overruled as to remaining causes of action. Although there is a substantial similarity between this case and the other law suit, the parties and causes of action are not identical. Cross-defendant may raise the issue of another action pending as an affirmative defense. Further, the Court recommends that parties seek single assignment. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
